senses.definition: an abnormal medical sign where sounds from inside the lungs are heard more clearly through the chest when a doctor listens
examples: The doctor heard bronchiloquy during the lung exam.
Bronchiloquy can be a sign of a lung problem.

senses.definition: a mucus-filled segment of a bronchus (airway) that is blocked so the mucus cannot drain
examples: Doctors found a bronchiocele on imaging.
A bronchiocele forms when mucus becomes trapped in a blocked bronchus.

senses.definition: a term that appears to be a misspelling or uncommon variant of a respiratory condition related to the bronchi; exact meaning is unclear
examples: The word 'bronchiocrisis' is not commonly used in standard medical English.
You may mean another term; check the spelling.

senses.definition: related to a disease, especially cancer, that starts in the bronchi (main airways)
examples: The report said the cancer was bronchiogenic.
Bronchiogenic tumors begin in the bronchi.

senses.definition: related to bronchioles (very small airways in the lungs)
examples: They described bronchiolar inflammation.
Damage to bronchiolar tissue can affect breathing.

senses.definition: a very small airway in the lungs that carries air from the bronchi to the tiny air sacs
examples: Bronchioles carry air deeper into the lungs.
The infection spread to the bronchioles.

senses.definition: the small airways in the lungs that connect bronchi to the air sacs
examples: Bronchioles can become swollen during illness.
Asthma often affects the bronchioles.

senses.definition: a plural form used in some medical/Latin-based writing for bronchioles (small lung airways)
examples: The study used 'bronchioli' in its anatomical description.
Bronchioli can be affected in respiratory disease.

senses.definition: inflammation of the bronchioles that often causes coughing and breathing difficulty, especially in children
examples: Bronchiolitis is common during cold seasons.
My baby was diagnosed with bronchiolitis.

senses.definition: to cause bronchiolitis; to infect or inflame the bronchioles
examples: The virus bronchiolitises many infants.
In severe cases, infection can bronchiolitises the airways.

senses.definition: a single bronchiole (a small airway in the lungs); the term is less common than bronchiole
examples: The older text described the bronchiolus.
Bronchiolus narrowing can affect airflow.

senses.definition: tightening of the muscles around the bronchi or airways that makes breathing harder
examples: Bronchiospasm caused wheezing.
During an asthma attack, bronchiospasm can occur.

senses.definition: narrowing of a bronchus that can reduce airflow
examples: Bronchiostenosis was seen on the scan.
Bronchiostenosis can make breathing more difficult.

senses.definition: an uncommon variant referring to bronchitis (inflammation of the bronchial tubes)
examples: Some texts use 'bronchites' for bronchitis.
The patient had symptoms described as bronchites.

senses.definition: related to bronchitis, or causing symptoms like bronchitis (cough and airway inflammation)
examples: She has a bronchitic cough.
Bronchitic symptoms often improve with time.

senses.definition: a person who has bronchitis, or people described as having bronchitis
examples: Doctors treated several bronchitics during the outbreak.
Bronchitics often experience persistent coughing.

senses.definition: an uncommon plural/variant form used to refer to cases of bronchitis
examples: Older medical writing used bronchitides.
Bronchitides can involve persistent airway inflammation.

senses.definition: inflammation of the bronchial tubes, causing cough and difficulty breathing
examples: Bronchitis often starts after a cold.
He was diagnosed with bronchitis and prescribed treatment.

senses.definition: to cause bronchitis; to infect or inflame the bronchial tubes
examples: The infection bronchitises the airways.
A virus can bronchitises a person and lead to coughing.

senses.definition: an anatomical term used in some medical writing for a bronchus (one of the main airways in the lungs)
examples: The anatomy book used 'bronchium' to refer to a bronchus.
Bronchium narrowing can reduce airflow.

senses.definition: a word part meaning 'bronchus' or 'airways of the lungs'
examples: In bronchitis, 'broncho-' relates to the airways.
Broncho- means the lungs' main air passages.

senses.definition: inflammation of the lymph nodes near the bronchi (airways), often caused by infection
examples: Bronchoadenitis can happen with certain infections.
The scan suggested bronchoadenitis near the bronchi.

senses.definition: relating to both the bronchi (airways) and the alveoli (air sacs)
examples: The doctor mentioned a bronchoalveolar test.
A bronchoalveolar process involves the airways and air sacs.

senses.definition: a lung infection or inflammation caused by a fungus (Aspergillus) that affects the bronchi/airways
examples: Bronchoaspergillosis can occur in people with lung disease.
The test confirmed bronchoaspergillosis.

senses.definition: a condition where the bronchi produce a lot of thick mucus
examples: Bronchoblennorrhea involves heavy mucus production in the bronchi.
Doctors looked for bronchoblennorrhea because of the patient’s mucus.
